{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_6d2f4b1d41cd41db9c3206f3","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Pluck strand: primary bead selection from the assigned workspace.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Pluck handles >90% of all bead processing. It queries the bead store for\n4\t//! unassigned, ready beads, filters by excluded labels, and sorts them in\n5\t//! deterministic priority order: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! Given the same queue state, every worker computes the same candidate list.\n8\t\n9\tuse crate::bead_store::{BeadStore, Filters};\n10\tuse crate::types::{Bead, StrandError, StrandResult};\n11\t\n12\t/// Default labels excluded from Pluck selection when not configured.\n13\tconst DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS: &[&str] = &[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"];\n14\t\n15\t/// The Pluck strand — primary work selection.\n16\tpub struct PluckStrand {\n17\t    /// Labels to exclude from candidate selection.\n18\t    exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n19\t    /// Auto-split beads after this many consecutive failures (0 = disabled).\n20\t    split_after_failures: u32,\n21\t}\n22\t\n23\timpl PluckStrand {\n24\t    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels.\n25\t    ///\n26\t    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n27\t    /// `blocked`) is used.\n28\t    pub fn new(exclude_labels: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n29\t        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n30\t            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n31\t                .iter()\n32\t                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n33\t                .collect()\n34\t        } else {\n35\t            exclude_labels\n36\t        };\n37\t        PluckStrand {\n38\t            exclude_labels: labels,\n39\t            split_after_failures: 3, // default threshold\n40\t        }\n41\t    }\n42\t\n43\t    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels and split threshold.\n44\t    ///\n45\t    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n46\t    /// `blocked`) is used.\n47\t    pub fn with_split_threshold(exclude_labels: Vec<String>, split_after_failures: u32) -> Self {\n48\t        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n49\t            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n50\t                .iter()\n51\t                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n52\t                .collect()\n53\t        } else {\n54\t            exclude_labels\n55\t        };\n56\t        PluckStrand {\n57\t            exclude_labels: labels,\n58\t            split_after_failures,\n59\t        }\n60\t    }\n61\t\n62\t    /// Extract the failure count from a bead's labels.\n63\t    ///\n64\t    /// Labels follow the pattern `failure-count:N`. Returns the count if found,\n65\t    /// or 0 if no failure-count label exists.\n66\t    fn extract_failure_count(bead: &Bead) -> u32 {\n67\t        bead.labels\n68\t            .iter()\n69\t            .filter_map(|l| l.strip_prefix(\"failure-count:\"))\n70\t            .filter_map(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())\n71\t            .max()\n72\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n73\t    }\n74\t\n75\t    /// Sort candidates in deterministic priority order.\n76\t    ///\n77\t    /// Sort key: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n78\t    /// The id tie-breaker ensures identical ordering across platforms.\n79\t    fn sort_candidates(candidates: &mut [Bead]) {\n80\t        candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n81\t            a.priority\n82\t                .cmp(&b.priority)\n83\t                .then_with(|| a.created_at.cmp(&b.created_at))\n84\t                .then_with(|| a.id.as_ref().cmp(b.id.as_ref()))\n85\t        });\n86\t    }\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\t#[async_trait::async_trait]\n90\timpl super::Strand for PluckStrand {\n91\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n92\t        \"pluck\"\n93\t    }\n94\t\n95\t    #[tracing::instrument(\n96\t        name = \"strand.pluck\",\n97\t        skip(self, store),\n98\t        fields(\n99\t            strand = \"pluck\",\n100\t            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n101\t            split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n102\t        )\n103\t    )]\n104\t    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n105\t        tracing::debug!(\n106\t            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n107\t            split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n108\t            \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\"\n109\t        );\n110\t\n111\t        // 1. Query bead store for ready, unassigned beads.\n112\t        let filters = Filters {\n113\t            assignee: None,\n114\t            exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n115\t        };\n116\t\n117\t        tracing::debug!(\n118\t            filters = ?filters,\n119\t            \"Querying bead store for ready candidates\"\n120\t        );\n121\t\n122\t        let mut candidates = match store.ready(&filters).await {\n123\t            Ok(beads) => {\n124\t                tracing::debug!(\n125\t                    count = beads.len(),\n126\t                    \"Bead store returned {} candidates\",\n127\t                    beads.len()\n128\t                );\n129\t                beads\n130\t            }\n131\t            Err(e) => {\n132\t                tracing::error!(\n133\t                    error = %e,\n134\t                    \"Bead store query failed\"\n135\t                );\n136\t                // Bead store error is semantically different from NoWork.\n137\t                return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n138\t            }\n139\t        };\n140\t\n141\t        // 2. Filter: remove beads with excluded labels.\n142\t        //    Defensive guard — store.ready() passes exclude_labels in its Filters,\n143\t        //    but the backing CLI may not include label data in every query type.\n144\t        //    Filtering here guarantees excluded-label beads are never presented as\n145\t        //    candidates regardless of backend behaviour, preventing the\n146\t        //    SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop observed when br ready --json\n147\t        //    omits label fields for some beads.\n148\t        let before_label_filter = candidates.len();\n149\t        candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n150\t        let after_label_filter = candidates.len();\n151\t\n152\t        if before_label_filter != after_label_filter {\n153\t            tracing::debug!(\n154\t                excluded_count = before_label_filter - after_label_filter,\n155\t                remaining = after_label_filter,\n156\t                excluded_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n157\t                \"Label filtering excluded {} beads\",\n158\t                before_label_filter - after_label_filter\n159\t            );\n160\t\n161\t            // Log each excluded bead at DEBUG level\n162\t            let excluded_beads: Vec<_> = candidates.iter()\n163\t                .filter(|b| b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)))\n164\t                .map(|b| (b.id.as_ref(), b.labels.clone()))\n165\t                .collect();\n166\t            for (id, labels) in excluded_beads {\n167\t                let excluded_reasons: Vec<_> = labels.iter()\n168\t                    .filter(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l))\n169\t                    .map(|l| l.as_str())\n170\t                    .collect();\n171\t                tracing::debug!(\n172\t                    bead_id = %id,\n173\t                    labels = ?labels,\n174\t                    excluded_reasons = ?excluded_reasons,\n175\t                    \"Excluded bead due to labels\"\n176\t                );\n177\t            }\n178\t\n179\t            // Re-filter since we logged them\n180\t            candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n181\t        } else {\n182\t            tracing::debug!(\n183\t                count = after_label_filter,\n184\t                \"No beads excluded by label filter\"\n185\t            );\n186\t        }\n187\t\n188\t        // 3. Filter: remove beads that are actively in_progress (claimed by another worker)\n189\t        //    and Open beads with a stale assignee. These are never claimable — the claimer\n190\t        //    will reject them every time, causing a hot loop.\n191\t        let before_status_filter = candidates.len();\n192\t        candidates.retain(|b| {\n193\t            !(matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress)\n194\t                || (b.status == crate::types::BeadStatus::Open && b.assignee.is_some()))\n195\t        });\n196\t        let after_status_filter = candidates.len();\n197\t\n198\t        if before_status_filter != after_status_filter {\n199\t            tracing::debug!(\n200\t                filtered_count = before_status_filter - after_status_filter,\n201\t                remaining = after_status_filter,\n202\t                \"Status/assignee filtering removed {} beads\",\n203\t                before_status_filter - after_status_filter\n204\t            );\n205\t        } else {\n206\t            tracing::debug!(\n207\t                count = after_status_filter,\n208\t                \"No beads excluded by status/assignee filter\"\n209\t            );\n210\t        }\n211\t\n212\t        // 4. Sort: deterministic (priority, created_at, id).\n213\t        if !candidates.is_empty() {\n214\t            let first = &candidates[0];\n215\t            tracing::debug!(\n216\t                total = candidates.len(),\n217\t                first_bead_id = %first.id,\n218\t                first_priority = first.priority,\n219\t                first_created_at = %first.created_at,\n220\t                \"Sorting {} candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\",\n221\t                candidates.len()\n222\t            );\n223\t        }\n224\t        Self::sort_candidates(&mut candidates);\n225\t\n226\t        // 5. Check for split trigger: if the first candidate has accumulated\n227\t        //    enough consecutive failures, dispatch a SPLIT instruction instead\n228\t        //    of returning the bead for normal processing.\n229\t        if self.split_after_failures > 0 {\n230\t            if let Some(first_candidate) = candidates.first() {\n231\t                let failure_count = Self::extract_failure_count(first_candidate);\n232\t                tracing::debug!(\n233\t                    bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n234\t                    failure_count = failure_count,\n235\t                    threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n236\t                    split_triggered = failure_count >= self.split_after_failures,\n237\t                    \"Checking split trigger for first candidate\"\n238\t                );\n239\t\n240\t                if failure_count >= self.split_after_failures {\n241\t                    tracing::info!(\n242\t                        bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n243\t                        failure_count = failure_count,\n244\t                        threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n245\t                        \"Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\"\n246\t                    );\n247\t                    return StrandResult::Split(Box::new(first_candidate.clone()), failure_count);\n248\t                }\n249\t            }\n250\t        } else {\n251\t            tracing::debug!(\"Split trigger disabled (threshold = 0)\");\n252\t        }\n253\t\n254\t        // 6. Return result.\n255\t        if candidates.is_empty() {\n256\t            tracing::debug!(\"No candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\");\n257\t            StrandResult::NoWork\n258\t        } else {\n259\t            let candidate_ids: Vec<&str> = candidates.iter()\n260\t                .map(|b| b.id.as_ref())\n261\t                .collect();\n262\t            tracing::info!(\n263\t                count = candidates.len(),\n264\t                candidates = ?candidate_ids,\n265\t                \"Returning {} candidates for processing\",\n266\t                candidates.len()\n267\t            );\n268\t            StrandResult::BeadFound(candidates)\n269\t        }\n270\t    }\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t// ─── Unit tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n274\t\n275\t#[cfg(test)]\n276\tmod tests {\n277\t    use super::*;\n278\t    use crate::bead_store::RepairReport;\n279\t    use crate::types::{BeadId, BeadStatus, ClaimResult};\n280\t\n281\t    use anyhow::Result;\n282\t    use chrono::{TimeZone, Utc};\n283\t    use std::path::PathBuf;\n284\t\n285\t    /// In-memory bead store for testing.\n286\t    struct MemoryStore {\n287\t        beads: Vec<Bead>,\n288\t    }\n289\t\n290\t    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n291\t    impl BeadStore for MemoryStore {\n292\t        async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n293\t            Ok(self.beads.clone())\n294\t        }\n295\t        async fn ready(&self, filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n296\t            let result: Vec<Bead> = self\n297\t                .beads\n298\t                .iter()\n299\t                .filter(|b| {\n300\t                    // Filter by assignee if set.\n301\t                    if let Some(ref a) = filters.assignee {\n302\t                        if b.assignee.as_ref() != Some(a) {\n303\t                            return false;\n304\t                        }\n305\t                    }\n306\t                    // Filter out beads with excluded labels.\n307\t                    if b.labels.iter().any(|l| filters.exclude_labels.contains(l)) {\n308\t                        return false;\n309\t                    }\n310\t                    true\n311\t                })\n312\t                .cloned()\n313\t                .collect();\n314\t Pluck strand: primary bead selection from the assigned workspace.\n//!\n//! Pluck handles >90% of all bead processing. It queries the bead store for\n//! unassigned, ready beads, filters by excluded labels, and sorts them in\n//! deterministic priority order: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n//!\n//! Given the same queue state, every worker computes the same candidate list.\n\nuse crate::bead_store::{BeadStore, Filters};\nuse crate::types::{Bead, StrandError, StrandResult};\n\n/// Default labels excluded from Pluck selection when not configured.\nconst DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS: &[&str] = &[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"];\n\n/// The Pluck strand — primary work selection.\npub struct PluckStrand {\n    /// Labels to exclude from candidate selection.\n    exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n    /// Auto-split beads after this many consecutive failures (0 = disabled).\n    split_after_failures: u32,\n}\n\nimpl PluckStrand {\n    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels.\n    ///\n    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n    /// `blocked`) is used.\n    pub fn new(exclude_labels: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n                .iter()\n                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n                .collect()\n        } else {\n            exclude_labels\n        };\n        PluckStrand {\n            exclude_labels: labels,\n            split_after_failures: 3, // default threshold\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels and split threshold.\n    ///\n    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n    /// `blocked`) is used.\n    pub fn with_split_threshold(exclude_labels: Vec<String>, split_after_failures: u32) -> Self {\n        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n                .iter()\n                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n                .collect()\n        } else {\n            exclude_labels\n        };\n        PluckStrand {\n            exclude_labels: labels,\n            split_after_failures,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Extract the failure count from a bead's labels.\n    ///\n    /// Labels follow the pattern `failure-count:N`. Returns the count if found,\n    /// or 0 if no failure-count label exists.\n    fn extract_failure_count(bead: &Bead) -> u32 {\n        bead.labels\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|l| l.strip_prefix(\"failure-count:\"))\n            .filter_map(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())\n            .max()\n            .unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Sort candidates in deterministic priority order.\n    ///\n    /// Sort key: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n    /// The id tie-breaker ensures identical ordering across platforms.\n    fn sort_candidates(candidates: &mut [Bead]) {\n        candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n            a.priority\n                .cmp(&b.priority)\n                .then_with(|| a.created_at.cmp(&b.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| a.id.as_ref().cmp(b.id.as_ref()))\n        });\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl super::Strand for PluckStrand {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"pluck\"\n    }\n\n    #[tracing::instrument(\n        name = \"strand.pluck\",\n        skip(self, store),\n        fields(\n            strand = \"pluck\",\n            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n            split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n        )\n    )]\n    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n        tracing::debug!(\n            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n            split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n            \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\"\n        );\n\n        // 1. Query bead store for ready, unassigned beads.\n        let filters = Filters {\n            assignee: None,\n            exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n        };\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            filters = ?filters,\n            \"Querying bead store for ready candidates\"\n        );\n\n        let mut candidates = match store.ready(&filters).await {\n            Ok(beads) => {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    count = beads.len(),\n                    \"Bead store returned {} candidates\",\n                    beads.len()\n                );\n                beads\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"Bead store query failed\"\n                );\n                // Bead store error is semantically different from NoWork.\n                return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n            }\n        };\n\n        // 2. Filter: remove beads with excluded labels.\n        //    Defensive guard — store.ready() passes exclude_labels in its Filters,\n        //    but the backing CLI may not include label data in every query type.\n        //    Filtering here guarantees excluded-label beads are never presented as\n        //    candidates regardless of backend behaviour, preventing the\n        //    SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop observed when br ready --json\n        //    omits label fields for some beads.\n        let before_label_filter = candidates.len();\n        candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n        let after_label_filter = candidates.len();\n\n        if before_label_filter != after_label_filter {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                excluded_count = before_label_filter - after_label_filter,\n                remaining = after_label_filter,\n                excluded_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n                \"Label filtering excluded {} beads\",\n                before_label_filter - after_label_filter\n            );\n\n            // Log each excluded bead at DEBUG level\n            let excluded_beads: Vec<_> = candidates.iter()\n                .filter(|b| b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)))\n                .map(|b| (b.id.as_ref(), b.labels.clone()))\n                .collect();\n            for (id, labels) in excluded_beads {\n                let excluded_reasons: Vec<_> = labels.iter()\n                    .filter(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l))\n                    .map(|l| l.as_str())\n                    .collect();\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    bead_id = %id,\n                    labels = ?labels,\n                    excluded_reasons = ?excluded_reasons,\n                    \"Excluded bead due to labels\"\n                );\n            }\n\n            // Re-filter since we logged them\n            candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                count = after_label_filter,\n                \"No beads excluded by label filter\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // 3. Filter: remove beads that are actively in_progress (claimed by another worker)\n        //    and Open beads with a stale assignee. These are never claimable — the claimer\n        //    will reject them every time, causing a hot loop.\n        let before_status_filter = candidates.len();\n        candidates.retain(|b| {\n            !(matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress)\n                || (b.status == crate::types::BeadStatus::Open && b.assignee.is_some()))\n        });\n        let after_status_filter = candidates.len();\n\n        if before_status_filter != after_status_filter {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                filtered_count = before_status_filter - after_status_filter,\n                remaining = after_status_filter,\n                \"Status/assignee filtering removed {} beads\",\n                before_status_filter - after_status_filter\n            );\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                count = after_status_filter,\n                \"No beads excluded by status/assignee filter\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // 4. Sort: deterministic (priority, created_at, id).\n        if !candidates.is_empty() {\n            let first = &candidates[0];\n            tracing::debug!(\n                total = candidates.len(),\n                first_bead_id = %first.id,\n                first_priority = first.priority,\n                first_created_at = %first.created_at,\n                \"Sorting {} candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\",\n                candidates.len()\n            );\n        }\n        Self::sort_candidates(&mut candidates);\n\n        // 5. Check for split trigger: if the first candidate has accumulated\n        //    enough consecutive failures, dispatch a SPLIT instruction instead\n        //    of returning the bead for normal processing.\n        if self.split_after_failures > 0 {\n            if let Some(first_candidate) = candidates.first() {\n                let failure_count = Self::extract_failure_count(first_candidate);\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n                    failure_count = failure_count,\n                    threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n                    split_triggered = failure_count >= self.split_after_failures,\n                    \"Checking split trigger for first candidate\"\n                );\n\n                if failure_count >= self.split_after_failures {\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n                        failure_count = failure_count,\n                        threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n                        \"Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\"\n                    );\n                    return StrandResult::Split(Box::new(first_candidate.clone()), failure_count);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\"Split trigger disabled (threshold = 0)\");\n        }\n\n        // 6. Return result.\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            tracing::debug!(\"No candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\");\n            StrandResult::NoWork\n        } else {\n            let candidate_ids: Vec<&str> = candidates.iter()\n                .map(|b| b.id.as_ref())\n                .collect();\n            tracing::info!(\n                count = candidates.len(),\n                candidates = ?candidate_ids,\n                \"Returning {} candidates for processing\",\n                candidates.len()\n            );\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(candidates)\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ─── Unit tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::bead_store::RepairReport;\n    use crate::types::{BeadId, BeadStatus, ClaimResult};\n\n    use anyhow::Result;\n    use chrono::{TimeZone, Utc};\n    use std::path::PathBuf;\n\n    ///  .beads\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|b| {\n                    // Filter by assignee if set.\n                    if let Some(ref a) = filters.assignee {\n                        if b.assignee.as_ref() != Some(a) {\n                            return false;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Filter out beads with excluded labels.\n                    if b.labels.iter().any(|l| filters.exclude_labels.contains(l)) {\n                        return false;\n                    }\n                    true\n                })\n                .cloned()\n                .collect();\n            Ok(result)\n        }\n\n        async fn show(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n            self.beads\n                .iter()\n        bead\n    }\n\n    fn make_bead_with_assignee(id: &str, assignee: &str) -> Bead {\n        let mut bead = make_bead(id, 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\");\n        bead.assignee = ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn candidates_sorted_by_priority_then_created_at() {\n        let store = MemoryStore {\n            beads: vec![\n                make_bead(\"low-pri\", 2, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n                make_bead(\"high-pri\", 1, \"2026-01-02 00:00:00\"),\n tie_broken_by_bead_id() {\n        // Same priority, same created_at — tie broken by id (lexicographic).\n        let store = MemoryStore {\n            beads: vec![\n                make_bead(\"bbb\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n                make_bead(\"aaa\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n                make_bead(\"ccc\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n            ],\n        };\n\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n        match result {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n got: {other:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    // Filtering\n    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn beads_with_excluded_labels_are_filtered() {\n        let store = MemoryStore {\n            beads: vec![\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"deferred-bead\", 1, vec![\"deferred\"]),\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"human-bead\", 1, vec![\"human\"]),\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"blocked-bead\", 1, vec![\"blocked\"]),\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"normal-bead\", 1, vec![]),\n            ],\n        };\n\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]); // Uses default excludes\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n        match result {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n                assert_eq!(beads.len(), 1);\n                assert_eq!(beads[0].id.as_ref(), \"normal-bead\");\n            }\n            other => panic!(\"expected BeadFound, got: {other:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn custom_exclude_labels_override_defaults() {\n        let store = MemoryStore {\n            beads: vec![\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"deferred-bead\", 1, vec![\"deferred\"]),\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"custom-excluded\", 1, vec![\"wip\"]),\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"normal-bead\", 1, vec![]),\n            ],\n        };\n\n        // Custom excludes: only \"wip\" — \"deferred\" is NOT excluded.\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![\"wip\".to_string()]);\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n        match result {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n                let ids: Vec<&str> = beads.iter().map(|b| b.id.as_ref()).collect();\n                assert!(ids.contains(&\"deferred-bead\"));\n                assert!(ids.contains(&\"normal-bead\"));\n                assert!(!ids.contains(&\"custom-excluded\"));\n            }\n            other => panic!(\"expected BeadFound, got: {other:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    // These tests use UnfilteredStore, which returns all beads from ready()\n    // without applying label exclusion — simulating a backend (e.g. br ready\n    // --json) that omits label data.  They verify that the strand's own\n    // defensive retain catches excluded-label beads regardless of what the\n    // store returns, preventing the SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop.\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn strand_filters_excluded_labels_when_store_does_not() {\n        let store = UnfilteredStore {\n            beads: vec![\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"deferred-bead\", 1, vec![\"deferred\"]),\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"normal-bead\", 1, vec![]),\n            ],\n        };\n\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n        match result {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n                assert_eq!(beads.len(), 1);\n                assert_eq!(beads[0].id.as_ref(), \"normal-bead\");\n            }\n            other => panic!(\"expected BeadFound, got: {other:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn all_excluded_labels_returns_no_work_via_strand_filter() {\n        // When every candidate has an excluded label and the store doesn't\n        // filter them, the strand's own retain must produce an empty list\n        // and return NoWork — not BeadFound([]).  NoWork causes the worker\n        // to move to Exhausted rather than spinning the claim-retry loop.\n        let store = UnfilteredStore {\n            beads: vec![\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"deferred-1\", 1, vec![\"deferred\"]),\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"deferred-2\", 2, vec![\"deferred\", \"human\"]),\n            ],\n        };\n\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n        match result {\n            StrandResult::NoWork => {}\n            other => panic!(\"expected NoWork, got: {other:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn open_bead_with_stale_assignee_is_filtered() {\n        // An open bead with a leftover assignee from a previous claim is NOT claimable.\n        // The claimer would reject it every time, causing a hot loop, so we filter\n        // these beads out at the pluck stage.\n        let store = MemoryStore {\n            beads: vec![\n                make_bead_with_assignee(\"stale-assignee\", \"worker-1\"),\n                make_bead(\"unassigned\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n            ],\n        };\n\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n        match result {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n                assert_eq!(\n                    beads.len(),\n                    1,\n                    \"only unassigned open beads should be claimable\"\n                );\n                assert_eq!(beads[0].id.as_ref(), \"unassigned\");\n            }\n            other => panic!(\"expected BeadFound, got: {other:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    //        match result {\n            StrandResult::NoWork => {}\n            other => panic!(\"expected NoWork, got: {other:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn store_error_returns_error_not_no_work() {\n        let store = FailingStore;\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n        match #[tokio::test]\n    async fn same_queue_state_produces_same_ordering() {\n        // Run twice with the same input and verify identical output.\n        let beads = vec![\n            make_bead(\"z-bead\", 2, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n            make_bead(\"a-bead\", 1, \"2026-01-03 00:00:00\"),\n            make_bead(\"m-bead\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n            make_bead(\"m-bead-2\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n        ];\n\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n\n        let store1 = MemoryStore {\n            beads: beads.clone(),\n        };\n        let store2 = MemoryStore { beads };\n\n        let r1 = strand.evaluate(&store1).await;\n        let r2 = strand.evaluate(&store2).await;\n\n        let ids1: Vec<String> = match r1 {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(b) => b.iter().map(|b| b.id.to_string()).collect(),\n            _ => panic!(\"expected BeadFound\"),\n        };\n        let ids2: Vec<String> = match r2 {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(b) => b.iter().map(|b| b.id.to_string()).collect(),\n            _ => panic!(\"expected BeadFound\"),\n        };\n\n        assert_eq!(ids1, ids2, \"ordering must be deterministic\");\n        assert_eq!(ids1, vec![\"m-bead\", \"m-bead-2\", \"a-bead\", \"z-bead\"]);\n    }\n\n    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    // Name\n    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    #[test]\n    fn strand_name_is_pluck() {\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n #[tokio::test]\n    async fn split_triggered_when_failure_count_exceeds_threshold() {\n        let bead_with_failures = make_bead_with_labels(\"failing-bead\", 1, vec![\"failure-count:3\"]);\n the caller\n238\t    /// can create the correct bead store for remote beads found by Explore.\n239\t    /// The accompanying `String` is the name of the strand that produced the\n240\t    /// candidate.\n241\t    ///\n242\t    /// When a strand returns `WorkCreated`, the waterfall restarts from Pluck.\n243\t    /// A restart cap prevents infinite loops (e.g. a strand that always creates\n244\t    /// work without producing a claimable bead).\n245\t    pub async fn select(\n246\t        &self,\n247\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n248\t        exclusions: &HashSet<BeadId>,\n249\t    ) -> Result<SelectOutcome> {\n250\t        const MAX_RESTARTS: u32 = 3;\n251\t        let mut restarts = 0u32;\n252\t        let mut restart_triggers: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n253\t        let mut strand_evaluations: Vec<StrandEvaluation> = Vec::new();\n254\t\n255\t        'waterfall: loop {\n256\t            for strand in &self.strands {\n257\t                let strand_name = strand.name().to_string();\n258\t                let strand_span = tracing::info_span!(\n259\t                    \"strand.{}\",\n260\t                    strand_name,\n261\t                    needle.strand.name = %strand_name,\n262\t                );\n263\t                let strand_enter = strand_span.entered();\n264\t\n265\t                let start = Instant::now();\n266\t                let result = strand.evaluate(store).await;\n267\t                let elapsed_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n268\t\n269\t                // Record strand evaluation result as span attribute.\n270\t                let (result_str, should_record) = match &result {\n271\t                    StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n272\t                        let count = beads.len();\n273\t                        (\n274\t                            format!(\"{}({})\", strand_results::BEAD_FOUND, count),\n275\t                            count > 0,\n276\t                        )\n277\t                    }\n278\t                    StrandResult::WorkCreated => (strand_results::WORK_CREATED.to_string(), true),\n279\t                    StrandResult::NoWork => (strand_results::NO_WORK.to_string(), true),\n280\t                    StrandResult::Error(_) => (strand_results::ERROR.to_string(), true),\n281\t                    StrandResult::Split(_, _failure_count) => {\n282\t                        (format!(\"{}({})\", strand_results::BEAD_FOUND, 1), true)\n283\t                    }\n284\t                };\n285\t                tracing::Span::current().record(attrs::NEEDLE_STRAND_RESULT, &result_str);\n286\t                tracing::Span::current().record(attrs::NEEDLE_STRAND_DURATION_MS, elapsed_ms);\n287\t\n288\t                // Set strand span status: Error for strand errors, Ok for all other results\n289\t                if matches!(result, StrandResult::Error(_)) {\n290\t                    tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_code\", 2u64);\n291\t                    tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_description\", &result_str);\n292\t                }\n293\t\n294\t                // Only record evaluations that produced meaningful results.\n295\t                // Skip recording empty BeadFound results since they don't\n296\t                // represent actual strand activity.\n297\t                if should_record {\n298\t                    strand_evaluations.push(StrandEvaluation {\n299\t                        strand_name: strand_name.clone(),\n300\t                        result: result_str.clone(),\n301\t                        duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n302\t                    });\n303\t                }\n304\t\n305\t                match result {\n306\t                    StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n307\t                        // Filter out beads that are in the exclusion set (e.g.\n308\t                        // recently race-lost).  This prevents the waterfall from\n309\t                        // immediately re-selecting a bead that just lost a claim\n310\t                        // race to another worker.\n311\t                        let original_count = beads.len();\n312\t                        let filtered: Vec<Bead> = beads\n313\t                            .into_iter()\n314\t                            .filter(|b| !exclusions.contains(&b.id))\n315\t                            .collect();\n316\t                        let excluded_count = original_count.saturating_sub(filtered.len());\n317\t\n318\t                        // Record queue depth for the Pluck strand (after filtering).\n319\t                        // This samples the current queue depth for the needle.queue.depth\n320\t                        // observable gauge, which is measured at strand evaluation.\n321\t                        // We report depth per priority level to enable filtered views.\n322\t                        if strand_name == \"pluck\" {\n323\t                            use std::collections::HashMap;\n324\t                            let mut depths: HashMap<u8, u64> = HashMap::new();\n325\t                            for bead in &filtered {\n326\t                                *depths.entry(bead.priority).or_insert(0) += 1;\n327\t                            }\n328\t                            self.telemetry.record_queue_depth(depths);\n329\t                        }\n330\t\n331\t                        if let Err(e) =\n332\t                            self.telemetry\n333\t                                .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n334\t                                    strand_name: strand_name.clone(),\n335\t                                    result: \"bead_found\".to_string(),\n336\t                                    duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n337\t                                })\n338\t                        {\n339\t                            tracing::warn!(\n340\t                                strand = %strand_name,\n341\t                                error = %e,\n342\t                                \"failed to emit strand evaluated telemetry\"\n343\t                            );\n344\t                        }\n345\t                        tracing::info!(\n346\t                            strand = %strand_name,\n347\t                            candidates = filtered.len(),\n348\t                            excluded = excluded_count,\n349\t                            elapsed_ms,\n350\t                            \"strand found candidates\"\n351\t                        );\n352\t                        if let Some(bead) = filtered.into_iter().next() {\n353\t                            // Keep the strand span active through the bead lifecycle.\n354\t                            // The span guard will be dropped when the bead lifecycle ends.\n355\t                            return Ok(SelectOutcome {\n356\t                                bead: Some((bead, strand_name.clone())),\n357\t                                waterfall_restarts: restarts,\n358\t                                restart_triggers,\n359\t                                strand_evaluations,\n360\t                                strand_span_guard: Some(strand_enter),\n361\t                                split_failure_count: None,\n362\t                            });\n363\t                        }\n364\t                        continue;\n365\t                    }\n366\t                    StrandResult::Split(bead, failure_count) => {\n367\t                        let bead = *bead;\n368\t                        if let Err(e) =\n369\t                            self.telemetry\n370\t                                .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n371\t                                    strand_name: strand_name.clone(),\n372\t                                    result: \"split\".to_string(),\n373\t                                    duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n374\t                                })\n375\t                        {\n376\t                            tracing::warn!(\n377\t                                strand = %strand_name,\n378\t                                error = %e,\n379\t                                \"failed to emit strand evaluated telemetry\"\n380\t                            );\n381\t                        }\n382\t                        tracing::info!(\n383\t                            strand = %strand_name,\n384\t                            bead_id = %bead.id,\n385\t                            failure_count,\n386\t                            \"strand triggered split for bead with excessive failures\"\n387\t                        );\n388\t                        return Ok(SelectOutcome {\n389\t                            bead: Some((bead, strand_name.clone())),\n390\t                            waterfall_restarts: restarts,\n391\t                            restart_triggers,\n392\t                            strand_evaluations,\n393\t                            strand_span_guard: Some(strand_enter),\n394\t                            split_failure_count: Some(failure_count),\n395\t                        });\n396\t                    }\n397\t                    StrandResult::WorkCreated => {\n398\t                        if let Err(e) =\n399\t                            self.telemetry\n400\t                                .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n401\t                                    strand_name: strand_name.clone(),\n402\t                                    result: \"work_created\".to_string(),\n403\t                                    duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n404\t                                })\n405\t                        {\n406\t                            tracing::warn!(\n407\t                                strand = %strand_name,\n408\t                                error = %e,\n409\t                                \"failed to emit strand evaluated telemetry\"\n410\t                            );\n411\t                        }\n412\t                        restarts += 1;\n413\t                        restart_triggers.push(strand_name.clone());\n414\t                        if restarts > MAX_RESTARTS {\n415\t                            tracing::warn!(\n416\t                                strand = %strand_name,\n417\t                                max_restarts = MAX_RESTARTS,\n418\t                                \"waterfall restart cap reached, continuing to evaluate remaining strands\"\n419\t                            );\n420\t                            // Do NOT return None — continue evaluating remaining strands\n421\t                            // so every strand emits telemetry and the operator can see\n422\t                            // why the worker is idle.\n423\t                            continue;\n424\t                        }\n425\t                        tracing::info!(\n426\t                            strand = %strand_name,\n427\t                            elapsed_ms,\n428\t                            restart = restarts,\n429\t                            \"strand created new work, restarting waterfall\"\n430\t                        );\n431\t                        continue 'waterfall;\n432\t                    }\n433\t                    StrandResult::NoWork => {\n434\t                        if let Err(e) =\n435\t                            self.telemetry\n436\t                                .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n437\t                                    strand_name: strand_name.clone(),\n438\t                                    result: \"no_work\".to_string(),\n439\t                                    duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n440\t                                })\n441\t                        {\n442\t                            tracing::warn!(\n443\t                                strand = %strand_name,\n444\t                                error = %e,\n445\t                                \"failed to emit strand evaluated telemetry\"\n446\t                            );\n447\t                        }\n448\t                        tracing::info!(\n449\t                            strand = %strand_name,\n450\t                            elapsed_ms,\n451\t                            \"strand returned no work\"\n452\t                        );\n453\t                        continue;\n454\t                    }\n455\t                    StrandResult::Error(e) => {\n456\t                        if let Err(te) =\n457\t                            self.telemetry\n458\t                                .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n459\t                                    strand_name: strand_name.clone(),\n460\t                                    result: \"error\".to_string(),\n461\t                                    duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n462\t                                })\n463\t                        {\n464\t                            tracing::warn!(\n465\t                                strand = %strand_name,\n466\t                                error = %te,\n467\t                                \"failed to emit strand evaluated telemetry\"\n468\t                            );\n469\t                        }\n470\t                        tracing::warn!(\n471\t                            strand = %strand_name,\n472\t                            error = %e,\n473\t                            elapsed_ms,\n474\t                            \"strand error, continuing to next strand\"\n475\t                        );\n476\t                        continue;\n477\t                    }\n478\t                }\n479\t            }\n480\t            // All strands evaluated without finding work or triggering a restart.\n481\t            return Ok(SelectOutcome {\n482\t                bead: None,\n483\t                waterfall_restarts: restarts,\n484\t                restart_triggers,\n485\t                strand_evaluations,\n486\t                strand_span_guard: None,\n487\t                split_failure_count: None,\n488\t            });\n489\t        }\n490\t    }\n491\t\n492\t    /// Return the names of all configured strands (for telemetry/debugging).\n493\t    pub fn strand_names(&self) -> Vec<&str> {\n494\t        self.strands.iter().map(|s| s.name()).collect()\n495\t    }\n496\t}\n497\t\n498\t// ─── Unit tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n499\t\n500\t#[cfg(test)]\n501\tmod tests {\n502\t    use super::*;\n503\t    use     \"always-creates\"\n751\t            ]\n752\t        );\n753\t    }\n754\t\n755\t    /// Strand that increments an external counter each time it is evaluated.\n756\t    struct CountingStrand {\n757\t        name: &'static str,\n758\t        count: std::sync::Arc<std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32>,\n759\t        returns_work_created: bool,\n760\t    }\n761\t\n762\t    impl CountingStrand {\n763\t        fn no_work(\n764\t            name: &'static str,\n765\t            count: std::sync::Arc<std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32>,\n766\t        ) -> Self {\n767\t            CountingStrand {\n768\t                name,\n769\t                count,\n770\t                returns_work_created: false,\n771\t            }\n772\t        }\n773\t\n774\t        fn work_created(\n775\t            name: &'static str,\n776\t            count: std::sync::Arc<std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32>,\n777\t        ) -> Self {\n778\t            CountingStrand {\n779\t                name,\n780\t                count,\n781\t                returns_work_created: true,\n782\t            }\n783\t        }\n784\t    }\n785\t\n786\t    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n787\t    impl Strand for CountingStrand {\n788\t        fn name(&self) -> &str {\n789\t            self.name\n790\t        }\n791\t\n792\t        async fn evaluate(&self, _store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n793\t            self.count.fetch_add(1, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n794\t            if self.returns_work_created {\n795\t                StrandResult::WorkCreated\n796\t            } else {\n797\t                StrandResult::NoWork\n798\t            }\n799\t        }\n800\t    }\n801\t\n802\t    #[tokio::test]\n803\t    async fn restart_cap_still_evaluates_remaining_strands() {\n804\t        // When a strand repeatedly returns WorkCreated, the waterfall restarts.\n805\t        // After MAX_RESTARTS, the remaining strands should still be evaluated\n806\t        // so that operators see telemetry for every strand in the cycle.\n807\t        let creator_count = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n808\t        let observer_a_count = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n809\t        let observer_b_count = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n810\t\n811\t        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![\n812\t            Box::new(CountingStrand::work_created(\n813\t                \"creator\",\n814\t                creator_count.clone(),\n815\t            )),\n816\t            Box::new(CountingStrand::no_work(\n817\t                \"observer-a\",\n818\t                observer_a_count.clone(),\n819\t            )),\n820\t            Box::new(CountingStrand::no_work(\n821\t                \"observer-b\",\n822\t                observer_b_count.clone(),\n823\t            )),\n824\t        ]);\n825\t        let store = EmptyStore;\n826\t        let exclusions = HashSet::new();\n827\t        let outcome = runner.select(&store, &exclusions).await.unwrap();\n828\t        assert!(outcome.bead.is_none());\n829\t\n830\t        // creator should have been evaluated MAX_RESTARTS + 1 = 4 times.\n831\t        assert_eq!(creator_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst), 4);\n832\t\n833\t        // After the restart cap, the remaining strands should each be evaluated\n834\t        // at least once (the final pass through the waterfall).\n835\t        let a = observer_a_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n836\t        let b = observer_b_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n837\t        assert!(\n838\t            a >= 1,\n839\t            \"observer_a should be evaluated at least once, got {a}\"\n840\t        );\n841\t        assert!(\n842\t            b >= 1,\n843\t            \"observer_b should be evaluated at least once, got {b}\"\n844\t        );\n845\t\n846\t        // All 4 WorkCreated events are captured in restart_triggers.\n847\t        assert_eq!(outcome.waterfall_restarts, 4);\n848\t        assert_eq!(outcome.restart_triggers.len(), 4);\n849\t        assert!(outcome.restart_triggers.iter().all(|t| t == \"creator\"));\n850\t    }\n851\t\n852\t    #[tokio::test]\n853\t    async fn empty_bead_found_continues_to_next() {\n854\t        let bead = Strand waterfall: ordered sequence of selection strategies.\n//!\n//! The StrandRunner evaluates strands in priority order. The first strand\n//! that yields a candidate wins. Strands are stateless — they receive queue\n//! state and return a candidate or nothing.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `types`, `config`, `bead_store`.\n\nmod explore;\nmod knot;\npub mod mend;\nmod pluck;\npub mod pulse;\npub mod reflect;\npub mod splice;\npub mod unravel;\npub mod weave;\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::time::Instant;\n\nuse anyhow::Result;\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::config::Config;\nuse crate::span::{attrs, strand_results};\nuse crate::types::{Bead, BeadId, StrandResult};\n\n/// A single strand evaluation result.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct StrandEvaluation {\n    pub strand_name: String,\n    pub result: String,\n    pub duration_ms: u64,\n}\n\n/// Result of a single `StrandRunner::select()` call.\n///\n/// Carries both the winning candidate (if any) and diagnostic statistics\n/// about restarts that occurred during the waterfall — used to populate\n/// `worker.exhausted` telemetry with a per-iteration breakdown.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct SelectOutcome {\n    /// The candidate bead and the strand that found it, or `None` if\n    /// all strands returned `NoWork`.\n    pub bead: Option<(Bead, String)>,\n    /// How many times the waterfall restarted from Pluck (cap = `MAX_RESTARTS`).\n    pub waterfall_restarts: u32,\n    /// Names of strands that returned `WorkCreated` and triggered a restart.\n    /// Duplicate entries are preserved (one per restart event).\n    pub restart_triggers: Vec<String>,\n    /// All strand evaluations in order, across all waterfall passes.\n    /// Each entry is (strand_name, result, duration_ms).\n    pub strand_evaluations: Vec<StrandEvaluation>,\n    /// The strand span guard for the strand that found the bead.\n    /// This keeps the strand span active through the bead lifecycle.\n    /// Dropped when the bead lifecycle ends (in do_log).\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub strand_span_guard: Option<tracing::span::EnteredSpan>,\n    /// If set, this bead should be dispatched with a split prompt instead of\n    /// the normal work prompt. Contains the consecutive failure count.\n    pub split_failure_count: Option<u32>,\n}\n\npub use explore::ExploreStrand;\npub use knot::KnotStrand;\npub use mend::{cleanup_orphaned_in_progress, MendStrand};\npub use pluck::PluckStrand;\npub use pulse::PulseStrand;\npub use reflect::{CliReflectAgent, ReflectAgent, ReflectStrand};\npub use splice::SpliceStrand;\npub use unravel::{UnravelAgent, UnravelStrand};\npub use weave::{CliWeaveAgent, WeaveAgent, WeaveStrand};\n\n/// A single selection strategy in the waterfall.\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\npub trait Strand: Send + Sync {\n    /// Human-readable name for telemetry.\n    fn name(&self) -> &str;\n\n    /// Evaluate this strand against the current queue state.\n    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult;\n}\n\n/// Runs strands in order, returning the first candidate found.\npub struct StrandRunner {\n    strands: Vec<Box<dyn Strand>>,\n    telemetry: crate::telemetry::Telemetry,\n}\n\nimpl StrandRunner {\n    pub fn new(strands: Vec<Box<dyn Strand>>) -> Self {\n        StrandRunner {\n            strands,\n            telemetry: crate::telemetry::Telemetry::new(\"strand-runner\".to_string()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Build the default strand waterfall from config.\n    ///\n    /// The waterfall order is:\n    /// Pluck → Mend → Explore → Weave → Unravel → Pulse → Reflect → Splice → Knot.\n    pub fn from_config(\n        config: &Config,\n        worker_id: &str,\n        registry: crate::registry::Registry,\n        telemetry: crate::telemetry::Telemetry,\n    ) -> Self {\n        let pluck = PluckStrand::with_split_threshold(\n            config.strands.pluck.exclude_labels.clone(),\n            config.strands.pluck.split_after_failures,\n        );\n\n        let heartbeat_dir = config.workspace.home.join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n        let heartbeat_ttl = std::time::Duration::from_secs(config.health.heartbeat_ttl_secs);\n        let lock_dir = std::path::PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\");\n        let log_dir = config\n            .telemetry\n            .file_sink\n            .log_dir\n            .clone()\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| config.workspace.home.join(\"logs\"));\n        let retention_days = config.telemetry.file_sink.retention_days;\n        let traces_dir = config.workspace.default.join(\".beads\").join(\"traces\");\n        let trace_retention_failed_days = config.strands.learning.trace_retention_failed_days;\n        let trace_retention_success_days = config.strands.learning.trace_retention_success_days;\n\n        // Create a new Registry instance pointing to the same path for ExploreStrand.\n        // We need to get the state_dir_for_explore before moving registry to MendStrand.\n        let state_fallback = config.workspace.home.join(\"state\");\n        let state_dir_for_explore = registry.path().parent().unwrap_or(&state_fallback);\n        let explore_registry = crate::registry::Registry::new(state_dir_for_explore);\n\n        let mend = MendStrand::new(\n            config.strands.mend.clone(),\n            heartbeat_dir,\n            heartbeat_ttl,\n            lock_dir,\n            worker_id.to_string(),\n            registry,\n            telemetry.clone(),\n            log_dir,\n            retention_days,\n            traces_dir,\n            trace_retention_failed_days,\n            trace_retention_success_days,\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            config.strands.learning.max_learnings,\n            config.workspace.home.join(\"state\"),\n            config.limits.clone(),\n        );\n\n        let state_base = config.workspace.home.join(\"state\");\n\n        let explore = ExploreStrand::new(\n            config.strands.explore.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            explore_registry,\n            telemetry.clone(),\n            worker_id.to_string(),\n        );\n\n        let weave = WeaveStrand::new(\n            config.strands.weave.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            state_base.join(\"weave\"),\n            Box::new(CliWeaveAgent::new(config.agent.default.clone())),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n\n        let unravel = UnravelStrand::new(\n            config.strands.unravel.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            state_base.join(\"unravel\"),\n            Box::new(unravel::CliUnravelAgent::new(config.agent.default.clone())),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n\n        let pulse = PulseStrand::new(\n            config.strands.pulse.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            state_base.join(\"pulse\"),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n\n        // Create the extraction agent if configured.\n        let reflect_agent = config\n            .strands\n            .reflect\n            .extraction_agent\n            .as_ref()\n            .map(|agent_cmd| {\n                Box::new(reflect::CliReflectAgent::new(\n                    agent_cmd.clone(),\n                    config.strands.reflect.extraction_prompt_template.clone(),\n                )) as Box<dyn reflect::ReflectAgent>\n            });\n\n        let reflect = ReflectStrand::new(\n            config.strands.reflect.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            state_base.join(\"reflect\"),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n            reflect_agent,\n        );\n\n        // Reconstruct heartbeat_dir for Splice (same path used by Mend).\n        let splice_heartbeat_dir = config.workspace.home.join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n        let runner_telemetry = telemetry.clone();\n        let splice = SpliceStrand::new(\n            config.strands.splice.clone(),\n            splice_heartbeat_dir,\n            state_base.join(\"splice\"),\n            telemetry,\n        );\n\n        let knot = KnotStrand::new(config.strands.knot.clone());\n        StrandRunner {\n            strands: vec![\n                Box::new(pluck),\n                Box::new(mend),\n                Box::new(explore),\n                Box::new(weave),\n                Box::new(unravel),\n                Box::new(pulse),\n                Box::new(reflect),\n                Box::new(splice),\n                Box::new(knot),\n            ],\n            telemetry: runner_telemetry,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run the waterfall, returning a `SelectOutcome` that carries the winning\n    /// candidate (if any) plus restart diagnostics.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the full `Bead` (including its workspace path) so the caller\n    /// can create the correct bead store for remote beads found by Explore.\n    /// The accompanying `String` is the name of the strand that produced the\n    /// candidate.\n    ///\n    /// When a strand returns `WorkCreated`, the waterfall restarts from Pluck.\n    /// A restart cap prevents infinite loops (e.g. a strand that always creates\n    /// work without producing a claimable bead).\n    pub async fn select(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        exclusions: &HashSet<BeadId>,\n    ) -> Result<SelectOutcome> {\n        const MAX_RESTARTS: u32 = 3;\n        let mut restarts = 0u32;\n        let mut restart_triggers: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n        let mut strand_evaluations: Vec<StrandEvaluation> = Vec::new();\n\n        'waterfall: loop {\n            for strand in &self.strands {\n                let strand_name = strand.name().to_string();\n                let strand_span = tracing::info_span!(\n                    \"strand.{}\",\n                    strand_name,\n                    needle.strand.name = %strand_name,\n                );\n                let strand_enter = strand_span.entered();\n\n                let start = Instant::now();\n                let result = strand.evaluate(store).await;\n                let elapsed_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n\n                // Record strand evaluation result as span attribute.\n                let (result_str, should_record) = match &result {\n                    StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n                        let count = beads.len();\n                        (\n                            format!(\"{}({})\", strand_results::BEAD_FOUND, count),\n                            count > 0,\n                        )\n                    }\n                    StrandResult::WorkCreated => (strand_results::WORK_CREATED.to_string(), true),\n                    StrandResult::NoWork => (strand_results::NO_WORK.to_string(), true),\n                    StrandResult::Error(_) => (strand_results::ERROR.to_string(), true),\n                    StrandResult::Split(_, _failure_count) => {\n                        (format!(\"{}({})\", strand_results::BEAD_FOUND, 1), true)\n                    }\n                };\n                tracing::Span::current().record(attrs::NEEDLE_STRAND_RESULT, &result_str);\n                tracing::Span::current().record(attrs::NEEDLE_STRAND_DURATION_MS, elapsed_ms);\n\n                // Set strand span status: Error for strand errors, Ok for all other results\n                if matches!(result, StrandResult::Error(_)) {\n                    tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_code\", 2u64);\n                    tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_description\", &result_str);\n                }\n\n                // Only record evaluations that produced meaningful results.\n                // Skip recording empty BeadFound results since they don't\n                // represent actual strand activity.\n                if should_record {\n                    strand_evaluations.push(StrandEvaluation {\n                        strand_name: strand_name.clone(),\n                        result: result_str.clone(),\n                        duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n                    });\n                }\n\n                match result {\n                    StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n                        // Filter out beads that are in the exclusion set (e.g.\n                        // recently race-lost).  This prevents the waterfall from\n                        // immediately re-selecting a bead that just lost a claim\n                        // race to another worker.\n                        let original_count = beads.len();\n                        let filtered: Vec<Bead> = beads\n                            .into_iter()\n                            .filter(|b| !exclusions.contains(&b.id))\n                            .collect();\n                        let excluded_count = original_count.saturating_sub(filtered.len());\n\n                        // Record queue depth for the Pluck strand (after filtering).\n                        // This samples the current queue depth for the needle.queue.depth\n                        // observable gauge, which is measured at strand evaluation.\n                        // We report depth per priority level to enable filtered views.\n                        if strand_name == \"pluck\" {\n                            use std::collections::HashMap;\n                            let mut depths: HashMap<u8, u64> = HashMap::new();\n                            for bead in &filtered {\n                                *depths.entry(bead.priority).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                            }\n                            self.telemetry.record_queue_depth(depths);\n                        }\n\n                        if let Err(e) =\n                            self.telemetry\n                                .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n                                    strand_name: strand_name.clone(),\n                                    result: \"bead_found\".to_string(),\n                                    duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n                                })\n                        {\n                            tracing::warn!(\n                                strand = %strand_name,\n                                error = %e,\n                                \"failed to emit strand evaluated telemetry\"\n                            );\n                        }\n                        tracing::info!(\n                            strand = %strand_name,\n                            candidates = filtered.len(),\n                            excluded = excluded_count,\n                            elapsed_ms,\n                            \"strand found candidates\"\n                        );\n                        if let Some(bead) = filtered.into_iter().next() {\n                            // Keep the strand span active through the bead lifecycle.\n                            // The span guard will be dropped when the bead lifecycle ends.\n                            return Ok(SelectOutcome {\n                                bead: Some((bead, strand_name.clone())),\n                                waterfall_restarts: restarts,\n                                restart_triggers,\n                                strand_evaluations,\n                                strand_span_guard: Some(strand_enter),\n                                split_failure_count: None,\n                            });\n                        }\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    StrandResult::Split(bead, failure_count) => {\n                        let bead = *bead;\n                        if let Err(e) =\n                            self.telemetry\n                                .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n                                    strand_name: strand_name.clone(),\n                                    result: \"split\".to_string(),\n                                    duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n                                })\n                        {\n                            tracing::warn!(\n                                strand = %strand_name,\n                                error = %e,\n                                \"failed to emit strand evaluated telemetry\"\n                            );\n                        }\n                        tracing::info!(\n                            strand = %strand_name,\n                            bead_id = %bead.id,\n                            failure_count,\n                            \"strand triggered split for bead with excessive failures\"\n                        );\n                        return Ok(SelectOutcome {\n                            bead: Some((bead, strand_name.clone())),\n                            waterfall_restarts: restarts,\n                            restart_triggers,\n                            strand_evaluations,\n                            strand_span_guard: Some(strand_enter),\n                            split_failure_count: Some(failure_count),\n                        });\n                    }\n                    StrandResult::WorkCreated => {\n                        if let Err(e) =\n                            self.telemetry\n                                .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n                                    strand_name: strand_name.clone(),\n                                    result: \"work_created\".to_string(),\n                                    duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n                                })\n                        {\n                            tracing::warn!(\n                                strand = %strand_name,\n                                error = %e,\n                                \"failed to emit strand evaluated telemetry\"\n                            );\n                        }\n                        restarts += 1;\n                        restart_triggers.push(strand_name.clone());\n                        if restarts > MAX_RESTARTS {\n                            tracing::warn!(\n                                strand = %strand_name,\n                                max_restarts = MAX_RESTARTS,\n                                \"waterfall restart cap reached, continuing to evaluate remaining strands\"\n                            );\n                            // Do NOT return None — continue evaluating remaining strands\n                            // so every strand emits telemetry and the operator can see\n                            // why the worker is idle.\n                            continue;\n                        }\n                        tracing::info!(\n                            strand = %strand_name,\n                            elapsed_ms,\n                            restart = restarts,\n                            \"strand created new work, restarting waterfall\"\n                        );\n                        continue 'waterfall;\n                    }\n                    StrandResult::NoWork => {\n                        if let Err(e) =\n                            self.telemetry\n                                .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n                                    strand_name: strand_name.clone(),\n                                    result: \"no_work\".to_string(),\n                                    duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n                                })\n                        {\n                            tracing::warn!(\n                                strand = %strand_name,\n                                error = %e,\n                                \"failed to emit strand evaluated telemetry\"\n                            );\n                        }\n                        tracing::info!(\n                            strand = %strand_name,\n                            elapsed_ms,\n                            \"strand returned no work\"\n                        );\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    StrandResult::Error(e) => {\n                        if let Err(te) =\n                            self.telemetry\n                                .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n                                    strand_name: strand_name.clone(),\n                                    result: \"error\".to_string(),\n                                    duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n                                })\n                        {\n                            tracing::warn!(\n                                strand = %strand_name,\n                                error = %te,\n                                \"failed to emit strand evaluated telemetry\"\n                            );\n                        }\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            strand = %strand_name,\n                            error = %e,\n                            elapsed_ms,\n                            \"strand error, continuing to next strand\"\n                        );\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            // All strands evaluated without finding work or triggering a restart.\n            return Ok(SelectOutcome {\n                bead: None,\n                waterfall_restarts: restarts,\n                restart_triggers,\n                strand_evaluations,\n                strand_span_guard: None,\n                split_failure_count: None,\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Return the names of all configured strands (for telemetry/debugging).\n    pub fn strand_names(&self) -> Vec<&str> {\n        self.strands.iter().map(|s| s.name()).collect()\n    }\n}\n\n// ─── Unit tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::types::{Bead, BeadId};\n\n    /// A stub strand that always returns the given result.\n    struct StubStrand {\n        name: &'static str,\n        result: std::sync::Mutex<Option<StrandResult>>,\n    }\n\n    impl StubStrand {\n        fn no_work(name: &'static str) -> Self {\n            StubStrand {\n                name,\n                result: std::sync::Mutex::new(Some(StrandResult::NoWork)),\n            }\n        }\n\n        fn beads(name: &'static str, beads: Vec<Bead>) -> Self {\n            StubStrand {\n                name,\n                result: std::sync::Mutex::new(Some(StrandResult::BeadFound(beads))),\n            }\n        }\n\n        fn work_created(name: &'static str) -> Self {\n            StubStrand {\n                name,\n                result: std::sync::Mutex::new(Some(StrandResult::WorkCreated)),\n            }\n        }\n\n        fn error(name: &'static str, msg: &str) -> Self {\n            StubStrand {\n                name,\n                result: std::sync::Mutex::new(Some(StrandResult::Error(\n                    crate::types::StrandError::ConfigError(msg.to_string()),\n                ))),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n    impl Strand for StubStrand {\n        fn name(&self) -> &str {\n            self.name\n        }\n\n        async fn evaluate(&self, _store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n            self.result\n                .lock()\n                .unwrap()\n                .take()\n                .unwrap_or(StrandResult::NoWork)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn make_test_bead(id: &str) -> Bead {\n        use chrono::Utc;\n        Bead {\n            id: BeadId::from(id.to_string()),\n            title: format!(\"Test bead {id}\"),\n            body: None,\n            priority: 1,\n            status: crate::types::BeadStatus::Open,\n            assignee: None,\n            labels: vec![],\n            workspace: std::path::PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n            dependencies: vec![],\n            dependents: vec![],\n            created_at: Utc::now(),\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Stub BeadStore for tests — always returns empty.\n    struct EmptyStore;\n\n    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n    impl BeadStore for EmptyStore {\n        async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn ready(&self, _filters: &crate::bead_store::Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn show(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not found\")\n        }\n        async fn claim(&self, _id: &BeadId, _actor: &str) -> Result<crate::types::ClaimResult> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not implemented\")\n        }\n        async fn release(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn flush(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn reopen(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn labels(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn create_bead(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str, _labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n #[tokio::test]\n    async fn first_strand_with_beads_wins() {\n        let bead = make_test_bead(\"test-001\");\n        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![\n            Box::new(StubStrand::no_work(\"empty\")),\n            Box::new(StubStrand::beads(\"finder\", vec![bead])),\n        ]);\n        let store = EmptyStore;\n        let outcome = runner.select(&store, &HashSet::new()).await.unwrap();\n        let (bead, strand_name) = outcome.bead.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(bead.id, BeadId::from(\"test-001\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(strand_name, \"finder\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn work_created_restarts_waterfall() {\n        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![\n            Box::new(StubStrand::work_created(\"creator\")),\n            Box::new(StubStrand::beads(\n                \"finder\",\n                vec![make_test_bead(\"test-002\")],\n            )),\n        ]);\n        let store = EmptyStore;\n        // WorkCreated restarts the waterfall. On the second pass, \"creator\"\n        // returns NoWork (stub consumed) and \"finder\" yields the bead.\n        let outcome = runner.select(&store, &HashSet::new()).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            outcome.bead.map(|(b, _)| b.id),\n            Some(BeadId::from(\"test-002\".to_string()))\n        );\n        assert_eq!(outcome.waterfall_restarts, 1);\n
